{{神社 |名称 = 塩屋八幡宮 |画像 = |所在地 = 熊本県八代市八幡町1-11 |位置 = |祭神 = 応神天皇
神宮皇后
仁徳天皇
武内宿禰 |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 1632年（寛永9年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 11月25日 |神事 = }} '''塩屋八幡宮'''（しおやはちまんぐう）は、[[熊本県]][[八代市]]八幡町にある[[神社]]である。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は村社。 == 由緒 == [[1632年]]（[[寛永]]9年）、[[細川忠興|細川忠興（三斎）]]が八代城に隠棲するに伴い、崇敬する[[宇佐神宮]]を勧請し八幡浜（現・若宮神社の地）に創建した塩屋八幡宮由緒書より。 [[1655年]]（[[明暦]]元年）、[[松井興長]]が現在の地に社を移し、八代城下西域の守護神とした。九州三大祭りの一つである[[八代神社]]の妙見祭において、神幸の御泊所となっている[[熊本日日新聞]]編纂・発行『熊本県大百科事典』、1982、398頁。 == 御祭神 == * [[応神天皇]] * [[神宮皇后]] * [[仁徳天皇]] * [[武内宿禰]] == 例祭日 == * [[11月25日]] == 境内社 == * [[金刀比羅宮]] :御祭神 - [[大物主命]]。[[崇徳天皇]]。 * [[稲荷神社]] :御祭神 - [[倉稲魂神]]。[[猿田彦命]]。大宮女命。 == 出典 == == 参考文献 == * 熊本県教育会・石川愛郷編纂『八代郡誌』1927、（1973年再発行、[[臨川書店]]） == 関連項目 == * [[宇佐神宮]] * [[八幡宮]] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しおやはちまんくう}} [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:熊本県の旧村社]] [[Category:八代市]]

{{神社 |名称 = 塩屋八幡宮 |画像 = |所在地 = 熊本県八代市八幡町1-11 |位置 = |祭神 = 応神天皇

仁徳天皇

武内宿禰 |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 1632年（寛永9年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 11月25日 |神事 = }} '''塩屋八幡宮'''（しおやはちまんぐう）は、[[熊本県]][[八代市]]八幡町にある[[神社]]である。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は村社。 == 由緒 == [[1632年]]（[[寛永]]9年）、[[細川忠興]]（三斎）が[[八代城]]に隠棲するに伴い、崇敬する[[宇佐神宮]]を勧請し八幡浜（現・若宮神社の地）に創建した塩屋八幡宮由緒書より。 [[1655年]]（[[明暦]]元年）、[[松井興長]]が現在の地に社を移し、八代城下西域の守護神とした。九州三大祭りの一つである[[八代神社]]の妙見祭において、神幸行列の御旅所となっている[[熊本日日新聞]]編纂・発行『熊本県大百科事典』、1982年、398頁。 == 祭神 == * [[応神天皇]] * [[神功皇后]] * [[仁徳天皇]] * [[武内宿禰]] == 例祭日 == * [[11月25日]] == 境内社 == * [[金刀比羅宮]] :祭神 - [[大物主|大物主命]]、[[崇徳天皇]] * [[稲荷神社]] :祭神 - [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]、[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]、大宮女命 == 出典 == == 参考文献 == * 熊本県教育会・石川愛郷編纂『八代郡誌』1927年、（1973年再発行、[[臨川書店]]） * 角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典43 熊本県』[[角川書店]]、1987年、534頁 == 関連項目 == * [[宇佐神宮]] * [[八幡宮]] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しおやはちまんくう}} [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:熊本県の旧村社]] [[Category:八代市の神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Shioya Hachiman-gū |image = [[File:Shioya-hachiman-shrine.jpg|270px]]
Shioya Hachiman-gū |location = Yatsushiro, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan |position = {{coord|32|30|17.3|N|130|35|36.3|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deities = Emperor Ōjin
Empress Jingū
Emperor Nintoku
Takeuchi no Sukune |status = Village shrine (former) |established = 1632 (Kan'ei 9) |main_building = |aliases = |annual_festival = November 25 |rituals = }} '''Shioya Hachiman-gū''' (塩屋八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yatsushiro, Kumamoto|ja|八代市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan. Under the former {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}, it was classified as a village shrine (村社). == History == In 1632 ({{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9), {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興}} (Sansai) retired to {{ill|Yatsushiro Castle|ja|八代城}} and, as part of his reverence for {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}}, he enshrined its deity at Hachiman-hama (present-day Wakamiya Shrine).From Shioya Hachiman-gū's historical documents. In 1655 ({{ill|Meireki|ja|明暦}} 1), {{ill|Matsui Okinaga|ja|松井興長}} relocated the shrine to its current site, establishing it as the guardian shrine of the western region of Yatsushiro's castle town. The shrine serves as the sacred resting place for the procession of the {{ill|Myōken Festival|ja|妙見祭}}, one of the three major festivals in Kyushu, associated with {{ill|Yatsushiro Shrine|ja|八代神社}}.Edited and published by Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun, "Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia," 1982, p. 398. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Takeuchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} == Annual Festival == * November 25 == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kompira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} * Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} * Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦命}}, Ōmiyame-no-Mikoto == References == == Further Reading == * Kumamoto Prefectural Education Society, edited by Aigō Ishikawa, "Yatsushiro County Gazetteer," 1927 (reprinted in 1973, Rinsho Shoten). * Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, "Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p. 534. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} {{Shintō horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yatsushiro]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yatsushiro]] [[Category:dardar]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shioya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:塩屋八幡宮]]

{{Shrine |name = Shioya Hachiman-gū |image = [[File:Shioya-hachiman-shrine.jpg|270px]]
Shioya Hachiman-gū |location = Yatsushiro, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan |position = {{coord|32|30|17.3|N|130|35|36.3|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deities = Emperor Ōjin
Empress Jingū
Emperor Nintoku
Takeuchi no Sukune |status = Village shrine (former) |established = 1632 (Kan'ei 9) |main_building = |aliases = |annual_festival = November 25 |rituals = }} '''Shioya Hachiman-gū''' (塩屋八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yatsushiro, Kumamoto|ja|八代市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan. Under the former {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}, it was classified as a village shrine (村社). == History == In 1632 ({{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9), {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興}} (Sansai) retired to {{ill|Yatsushiro Castle|ja|八代城}} and, as part of his reverence for {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}}, he enshrined its deity at Hachiman-hama (present-day Wakamiya Shrine).From Shioya Hachiman-gū's historical documents. In 1655 ({{ill|Meireki|ja|明暦}} 1), {{ill|Matsui Okinaga|ja|松井興長}} relocated the shrine to its current site, establishing it as the guardian shrine of the western region of Yatsushiro's castle town. The shrine serves as the sacred resting place for the procession of the {{ill|Myōken Festival|ja|妙見祭}}, one of the three major festivals in Kyushu, associated with {{ill|Yatsushiro Shrine|ja|八代神社}}.Edited and published by Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun, "Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia," 1982, p. 398. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Takeuchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} == Annual Festival == * November 25 == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kompira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} * Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} * Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦命}}, Ōmiyame-no-Mikoto == References == == Further Reading == * Kumamoto Prefectural Education Society, edited by Aigō Ishikawa, "Yatsushiro County Gazetteer," 1927 (reprinted in 1973, Rinsho Shoten). * Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, "Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p. 534. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} {{Shintō horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yatsushiro]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yatsushiro]] [[Category:dardar]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shioya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shioya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:塩屋八幡宮]]

The shrine serves as the sacred resting place for the procession of the {{ill|Myōken Festival|ja|妙見祭}}, one of the three major festivals in Kyushu, associated with {{ill|Yatsushiro Shrine|ja|八代神社}}.Edited and published by Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun, "Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia," 1982, p. 398. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Takeuchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} == festival == * November 25 == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kompira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} * Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} * Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦命}}, Ōmiyame-no-Mikoto == References == == Further Reading == * Kumamoto Prefectural Education Society, edited by Aigō Ishikawa, "Yatsushiro County Gazetteer," 1927 (reprinted in 1973, Rinsho Shoten). * Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, "Kadokawa Japan Place name Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p. 534. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} {{Shintō horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yatsushiro]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yatsushiro]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shioya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shioya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:塩屋八幡宮]]

Under the former {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a village shrine (村社). == History == In 1632 ({{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9), {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|ja|細川忠興}} (Sansai) retired to {{ill|Yatsushiro Castle|ja|八代城}} and, as part of his reverence for {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}}, he enshrined its deity at Hachiman-hama (present-day Wakamiya Shrine).From Shioya Hachiman-gū's historical documents. In 1655 ({{ill|Meireki|ja|明暦}} 1), {{ill|Matsui Okinaga|ja|松井興長}} relocated the shrine to its current site, establishing it as the guardian shrine of the western region of Yatsushiro's castle town. The shrine serves as the sacred resting place for the procession of the {{ill|Myōken Festival|ja|妙見祭}}, one of the three major festivals in Kyushu, associated with {{ill|Yatsushiro Shrine|ja|八代神社}}.Edited and published by Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun, "Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia," 1982, p. 398. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Takeuchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} == festival == * November 25 == Sub-Shrines == * {{ill|Kompira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} * Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} * Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦命}}, Ōmiyame-no-Mikoto == References == == Further Reading == * Kumamoto Prefectural Education Society, edited by Aigō Ishikawa, "Yatsushiro County Gazetteer," 1927 (reprinted in 1973, Rinsho Shoten). * Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, "Kadokawa Japan Place name Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p. 534. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} {{Shintō horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yatsushiro]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yatsushiro]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shioya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shioya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:塩屋八幡宮]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 塩屋八幡宮 |画像 = [[File:Shioya-hachiman-shrine.jpg|270px]]
塩屋八幡宮 |所在地 = 熊本県八代市八幡町1-11 |位置 = {{coord|32|30|17.3|N|130|35|36.3|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 応神天皇
神宮皇后
仁徳天皇
武内宿禰 |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 1632年（寛永9年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 11月25日 |神事 = }} '''塩屋八幡宮'''（しおやはちまんぐう）は、[[熊本県]][[八代市]]八幡町にある[[神社]]である。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は村社。 == 由緒 == [[1632年]]（[[寛永]]9年）、[[細川忠興]]（三斎）が[[八代城]]に隠棲するに伴い、崇敬する[[宇佐神宮]]を勧請し八幡浜（現・若宮神社の地）に創建した塩屋八幡宮由緒書より。 [[1655年]]（[[明暦]]元年）、[[松井興長]]が現在の地に社を移し、八代城下西域の守護神とした。九州三大祭りの一つである[[八代神社]]の妙見祭において、神幸行列の御旅所となっている[[熊本日日新聞]]編纂・発行『熊本県大百科事典』、1982年、398頁。 == 祭神 == * [[応神天皇]] * [[神功皇后]] * [[仁徳天皇]] * [[武内宿禰]] == 例祭日 == * [[11月25日]] == 境内社 == * [[金刀比羅宮]] :祭神 - [[大物主|大物主命]]、[[崇徳天皇]] * [[稲荷神社]] :祭神 - [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]、[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]、大宮女命 == 出典 == == 参考文献 == * 熊本県教育会・石川愛郷編纂『八代郡誌』1927年、（1973年再発行、[[臨川書店]]） * 角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典43 熊本県』[[角川書店]]、1987年、534頁 == 関連項目 == * [[宇佐神宮]] * [[八幡宮]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しおやはちまんくう}} [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:八代市の歴史]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:熊本県の旧村社]] [[Category:八代市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shioya Hachiman-gū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shioya-hachiman-shrine.jpg|270px]]
Shioya Hachiman-gū |location = Yatsushiro, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan |position = {{coord|32|30|17.3|N|130|35|36.3|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deities = Emperor Ōjin
Empress Jingū
Emperor Nintoku
Takeuchi no Sukune |status = Village shrine (former) |established = 1632 (Kan'ei 9) |main_building = |aliases = |annual_festival = November 25 |rituals = }} '''Shioya Hachiman-gū''' (塩屋八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yatsushiro|Yatsushiro, Kumamoto]], Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan. Under the former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a village shrine (村社). == History == In 1632 ([[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9), [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] (Sansai) retired to [[:en:Yatsushiro Castle|Yatsushiro Castle]] and, as part of his reverence for [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]], he enshrined its deity at Hachiman-hama (present-day Wakamiya Shrine).From Shioya Hachiman-gū's historical documents. In 1655 ([[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 1), [[:en:Matsui Okinaga|Matsui Okinaga]] relocated the shrine to its current site, establishing it as the guardian shrine of the western region of Yatsushiro's castle town. The shrine serves as the sacred resting place for the procession of the {{ill|Myōken Festival|ja|妙見祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Festival|qq=}}, one of the three major festivals in Kyushu, associated with {{ill|Yatsushiro Shrine|ja|八代神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsushiro Shrine|qq=}}.Edited and published by Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun, "Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia," 1982, p. 398. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]] == festival == * November 25 == Sub-Shrines == * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] ** Enshrined deities: [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]], [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] ** Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}, Ōmiyame-no-Mikoto == References == == Further Reading == * Kumamoto Prefectural Education Society, edited by Aigō Ishikawa, "Yatsushiro County Gazetteer," 1927 (reprinted in 1973, Rinsho Shoten). * Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Editorial Committee, "Kadokawa Japan Place name Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p. 534. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]] * [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] {{Shintō horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|塩屋八幡宮|version=66274936|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yatsushiro]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yatsushiro]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Shioya Hachiman Shrine]]}} [[sn:Shioya Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:塩屋八幡宮]]

Under the former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a village shrine (村社). == History == In 1632 ([[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9), [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] (Sansai) retired to [[:en:Yatsushiro Castle|Yatsushiro Castle]] and, as part of his reverence for [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]], he enshrined its deity at Hachiman-hama (present-day Wakamiya Shrine).From Shioya Hachiman-gū's historical documents. In 1655 ([[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 1), [[:en:Matsui Okinaga|Matsui Okinaga]] relocated the shrine to its current site, establishing it as the guardian shrine of the western region of Yatsushiro's castle town. The shrine serves as the sacred resting place for the procession of the {{ill|Myōken Festival|ja|妙見祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Under the former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a village shrine (村社). == History == In 1632 ([[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9), [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] (Sansai) retired to [[:en:Yatsushiro Castle|Yatsushiro Castle]] and, as part of his reverence for [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]], he enshrined its deity at Hachiman-hama (present-day Wakamiya Shrine).From Shioya Hachiman-gū's historical documents. In 1655 ([[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 1), [[:en:Matsui Okinaga|Matsui Okinaga]] relocated the shrine to its current site, establishing it as the guardian shrine of the western region of Yatsushiro's castle town. Takeuchi no Sukune |status = Village shrine (former) |established = 1632 (Kan'ei 9) |main_building = |aliases = |annual_festival = November 25 |rituals = }} '''Shioya Hachiman-gū''' (塩屋八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yatsushiro|Yatsushiro, Kumamoto]], Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan. Under the former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a village shrine (村社). == History == In 1632 ([[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9), [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] (Sansai) retired to [[:en:Yatsushiro Castle|Yatsushiro Castle]] and, as part of his reverence for [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]], he enshrined its deity at Hachiman-hama (present-day Wakamiya Shrine).From Shioya Hachiman-gū's historical documents. In 1655 ([[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 1), [[:en:Matsui Okinaga|Matsui Okinaga]] relocated the shrine to its current site, establishing it as the guardian shrine of the western region of Yatsushiro's castle town. Under the former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a village shrine (村社). == History == In 1632 ([[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9), [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] (Sansai) retired to [[:en:Yatsushiro Castle|Yatsushiro Castle]] and, as part of his reverence for [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]], he enshrined its deity at Hachiman-hama (present-day Wakamiya Shrine).From Shioya Hachiman-gū's historical documents. In 1655 ([[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 1), [[:en:Matsui Okinaga|Matsui Okinaga]] relocated the shrine to its current site, establishing it as the guardian shrine of the western region of Yatsushiro's castle town. Empress Jingū
Emperor Nintoku
Takeuchi no Sukune |status = Village shrine (former) |established = 1632 (Kan'ei 9) |main_building = |aliases = |annual_festival = November 25 |rituals = }} '''Shioya Hachiman-gū''' (塩屋八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yatsushiro|Yatsushiro, Kumamoto]], Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan. Under the former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a village shrine (村社). == History == In 1632 ([[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9), [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] (Sansai) retired to [[:en:Yatsushiro Castle|Yatsushiro Castle]] and, as part of his reverence for [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]], he enshrined its deity at Hachiman-hama (present-day Wakamiya Shrine).From Shioya Hachiman-gū's historical documents. In 1655 ([[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 1), [[:en:Matsui Okinaga|Matsui Okinaga]] relocated the shrine to its current site, establishing it as the guardian shrine of the western region of Yatsushiro's castle town. The shrine serves as the sacred resting place for the procession of the {{ill|Myōken Festival|ja|妙見祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八代神社|1=Yatsushiro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390558}}, one of the three major festivals in Kyushu, associated with {{ill|Yatsushiro Shrine|ja|八代神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsushiro Shrine|qq=}}.Edited and published by Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun, "Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia," 1982, p. 398. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]] == festival == * November 25 == Sub-Shrines == * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] ** Enshrined deities: [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]], [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] ** Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, Ōmiyame-no-Mikoto == References Takeuchi no Sukune |status = Village shrine (former) |established = 1632 (Kan'ei 9) |main_building = |aliases = |annual_festival = November 25 |rituals = }} '''Shioya Hachiman-gū''' (塩屋八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yatsushiro|Yatsushiro, Kumamoto]], Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan. Under the former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a village shrine (村社). == History == In 1632 ([[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9), [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] (Sansai) retired to [[:en:Yatsushiro Castle|Yatsushiro Castle]] and, as part of his reverence for [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]], he enshrined its deity at Hachiman-hama (present-day Wakamiya Shrine).From Shioya Hachiman-gū's historical documents. In 1655 ([[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 1), [[:en:Matsui Okinaga|Matsui Okinaga]] relocated the shrine to its current site, establishing it as the guardian shrine of the western region of Yatsushiro's castle town. The shrine serves as the sacred resting place for the procession of the {{ill|Myōken Festival|ja|妙見祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八代神社|1=Yatsushiro Under the former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a village shrine (村社). == History == In 1632 ([[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9), [[:en:Hosokawa Tadaoki|Hosokawa Tadaoki]] (Sansai) retired to [[:en:Yatsushiro Castle|Yatsushiro Castle]] and, as part of his reverence for [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]], he enshrined its deity at Hachiman-hama (present-day Wakamiya Shrine).From Shioya Hachiman-gū's historical documents. In 1655 ([[:en:Meireki|Meireki]] 1), [[:en:Matsui Okinaga|Matsui Okinaga]] relocated the shrine to its current site, establishing it as the guardian shrine of the western region of Yatsushiro's castle town. The shrine serves as the sacred resting place for the procession of the {{ill|Myōken Festival|ja|妙見祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八代神社|1=Yatsushiro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390558}}, one of the three major festivals in Kyushu, associated with {{ill|Yatsushiro Shrine|ja|八代神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsushiro Shrine|qq=}}.Edited and published by Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun, "Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia," 1982, p. 398. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]] == festival == * November 25 == Sub-Shrines == * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] ** Enshrined deities: [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]], [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] ** Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukanomitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki Shrine]] {{Shintō horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|塩屋八幡宮|version=66274936|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塩屋八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11428677}} [[Category:Buildings in Empress Jingū
Emperor Nintoku
Takeuchi no Sukune |status = Village shrine (former) |established = 1632 (Kan'ei 9) |main_building = |aliases = |annual_festival = November 25 |rituals = }} '''Shioya Hachiman-gū''' (塩屋八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yatsushiro|en|Yatsushiro|WD=Q734479|lt=Yatsushiro, Kumamoto}}, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan. Under the former {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a village shrine (村社). == History == In 1632 ({{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} 9), {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|en|Hosokawa Tadaoki|WD=Q1147154|lt=Hosokawa Tadaoki}} (Sansai) retired to {{ill|Yatsushiro Castle|en|Yatsushiro Castle|WD=Q11390511|lt=Yatsushiro Castle}} and, as part of his reverence for {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Jingū}}, he enshrined its deity at Hachiman-hama (present-day Wakamiya Shrine).From Shioya Hachiman-gū's historical documents. In 1655 ({{ill|Meireki|en|Meireki|WD=Q1192766|lt=Meireki}} 1), {{ill|Matsui Okinaga|en|Matsui Okinaga|WD=Q4352514|lt=Matsui Okinaga}} relocated the shrine to its current site, establishing it as the guardian shrine of the western region of Yatsushiro's castle town. The shrine serves as the sacred resting place for the procession of the {{ill|Myōken Festival|ja|妙見祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八代神社|1=Yatsushiro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390558}}, one of the three major festivals in Kyushu, associated with {{ill|Yatsushiro Shrine|ja|八代神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsushiro Shrine|qq=}}.Edited and published by Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun, "Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia," 1982, p. 398. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|en|Takenouchi no [[ja:塩屋八幡宮]] [[ja:塩屋八幡宮]] [[ja:塩屋八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shioya Hachiman-gū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shioya-hachiman-shrine.jpg|270px]]
Shioya Hachiman-gū |location = Yatsushiro, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan |position = {{coord|32|30|17.3|N|130|35|36.3|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deities = Emperor Ōjin
Empress Jingū
Emperor Nintoku
Takeuchi no Sukune |status = Village shrine (former) |established = 1632 (Kan'ei 9) |main_building = |aliases = |annual_festival = November 25 |rituals = }} '''Shioya Hachiman-gū''' (塩屋八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yatsushiro|en|Yatsushiro|WD=Q734479|lt=Yatsushiro, Kumamoto|1=Yatsushiro}}, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan. Under the former {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a village shrine (村社). == History == In 1632 ({{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} 9), {{ill|Hosokawa Tadaoki|en|Hosokawa Tadaoki|WD=Q1147154|lt=Hosokawa Tadaoki|1=Hosokawa Tadaoki}} (Sansai) retired to {{ill|Yatsushiro Castle|en|Yatsushiro Castle|WD=Q11390511|lt=Yatsushiro Castle|1=Yatsushiro Castle}} and, as part of his reverence for {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Jingū|1=Usa Jingū}}, he enshrined its deity at Hachiman-hama (present-day Wakamiya Shrine).From Shioya Hachiman-gū's historical documents. In 1655 ({{ill|Meireki|en|Meireki|WD=Q1192766|lt=Meireki|1=Meireki}} 1), {{ill|Matsui Okinaga|en|Matsui Okinaga|WD=Q4352514|lt=Matsui Okinaga|1=Matsui Okinaga}} relocated the shrine to its current site, establishing it as the guardian shrine of the western region of Yatsushiro's castle town. The shrine serves as the sacred resting place for the procession of the {{ill|Myōken Festival|ja|妙見祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八代神社|1=Yatsushiro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390558}}, one of the three major festivals in Kyushu, associated with {{ill|Yatsushiro Shrine|ja|八代神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsushiro Shrine|qq=}}.Edited and published by Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun, "Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia," 1982, p. 398. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|en|Takenouchi no Sukune|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} == festival == * November 25 == Sub-Shrines == *
